title: Mascara on Polyester
timeEstimate: 20–60 minutes; wax + carbon-pigment + waterproof film, polyester locks all under heat
stainChemistry: Mascara is wax + intense carbon-black or iron-oxide pigment + polymer film-formers + oils. Waterproof mascara adds silicone and acrylate polymers specifically engineered to resist water cleaning — that's the whole point of waterproof. Two cases that need different handling: (1) STANDARD mascara — responds to powder + Dawn + cool wash; (2) WATERPROOF mascara — needs an oil-based makeup remover or solvent step because water-based products can't penetrate the silicone barrier. Polyester adds the heat trap (locks oil and pigment under heat, yellows over time) and is OLEOPHILIC (grabs the wax + oil tight). The recurring trap is rubbing — carbon-black pigment is extremely fine and gets pushed into fiber surface texture by aggressive scrubbing, leaving a permanent gray shadow.
whyThisWorks: Powder preload absorbs the oil component. Dawn handles the wax + oil matrix. For waterproof mascara, an oil-based makeup remover (Neutrogena oil-free Makeup Remover, micellar water, or Bioderma) on a cloth dissolves the silicone film FIRST so Dawn can reach the pigment after. Cool wash + AIR-DRY locks nothing. The honest cap: long-wear / waterproof mascara on dyed polyester sometimes plateaus with a faint gray shadow.
defaultAssumption: Mascara smudge on a pillowcase, collar, sleeve, or under-eye contact transfer onto a polyester garment. Standard mascara = high-confidence. Waterproof / smudge-proof / 24-hour formula = oil-based remover step is mandatory. Hot-dried = often permanent gray shadow.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Identify whether it's standard or waterproof mascara. Waterproof / smudge-proof / 24-hour formulas need an oil-based remover step that standard formulas don't. If unknown, treat as waterproof — the oil-based step doesn't hurt standard mascara.

Scrape excess mascara off with a plastic spoon edge. Flat-edge work only. NEVER rub — carbon-black pigment is extremely fine and rubbing pushes it into polyester surface texture, leaving a permanent gray shadow.
Cover the spot with cornstarch or baking soda. Press gently. 10 min absorb. Soft-brush off.

spottingProtocol.agent: Oil-Based Makeup Remover for Waterproof Mascara
spottingProtocol.equipment: Neutrogena oil-free remover / micellar water / Bioderma on cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Waterproof mascara only. Apply makeup remover to a clean white cloth — never pour. Press the spot 5 min. The remover dissolves the silicone film so Dawn can reach the pigment after. Skip this step for confirmed standard mascara.
Generous Dawn directly on the spot. Soft toothbrush 10 min. Polyester is oleophilic — extended dwell needed. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

spottingProtocol.agent: OxiClean Cool Soak Plus Cool Wash
spottingProtocol.equipment: OxiClean per label, COOL water, washing machine
spottingProtocol.instruction: OxiClean cool soak 30 min. Cool machine wash with normal detergent. SKIP fabric softener. AIR-DRY.
AIR-DRY only. Inspect in daylight. Faint gray shadow = repeat Dawn pass before any heat. NEVER hot dryer until cleared — locks pigment into polyester.

steps: 1. Identify standard vs waterproof formula. If unknown, treat as waterproof.
4. Oil-based makeup remover for waterproof mascara — cloth tamp, 5 min.
5. Generous Dawn pretreat, cool, 10 min, soft toothbrush.
6. OxiClean cool soak 30 min + cool machine wash.
products: Oil-based makeup remover (Neutrogena, micellar water, Bioderma) for waterproof
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat locks oil and pigment on polyester
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets oil, locks carbon-black pigment, yellows polyester
NEVER fabric softener — coats fiber, blocks cleaning
Always scrape gently, NEVER rub — rubbing pushes carbon pigment into fiber
Waterproof mascara needs oil-based remover step BEFORE Dawn
Polyester is oleophilic — extended Dawn dwell needed
Powder preload BEFORE water — water-first spreads stain

homeSolutions.agent: Standard or Waterproof Mascara — That's the Fork
homeSolutions.instruction: Standard mascara responds to the usual oil-stain protocol. Waterproof mascara is engineered to resist water — it has a silicone barrier that Dawn can't get through. For waterproof formulas, you need an oil-based makeup remover step FIRST to dissolve the silicone film so Dawn can reach the pigment after. If you don't know which formula it is, treat as waterproof — the extra step doesn't hurt. Two hard rules on polyester: NO hot water and NO hot dryer until cleared.

homeSolutions.agent: Scrape Gently — Carbon Pigment Pushes In
homeSolutions.instruction: If there's a clump of mascara on the fabric, lift it off with a plastic spoon edge. Flat-edge work only. NEVER rub. Mascara's carbon-black pigment is extremely fine and rubbing pushes it into polyester's surface texture, leaving a permanent gray shadow.
Cover the spot with cornstarch or baking soda. Press gently. 10 minutes. Soft-brush off. Pulls bulk oil up before water spreads it.

homeSolutions.agent: Oil-Based Makeup Remover for Waterproof Mascara
homeSolutions.instruction: Waterproof mascara only. Apply Neutrogena oil-free makeup remover, micellar water, or Bioderma to a clean white cloth — never pour on the garment. Press the spot for 5 minutes. The remover dissolves the silicone film so Dawn can reach the pigment after. SKIP this step if you've confirmed it's standard mascara.
Polyester is oleophilic — it grabs the wax + oil in mascara tight. Apply a generous amount of Dawn dish soap. Work in with a soft toothbrush for 10 minutes. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

homeSolutions.agent: OxiClean Cool Soak and Cool Wash
homeSolutions.instruction: Mix OxiClean per label in COOL water, soak 30 minutes. Cool machine wash with normal detergent. SKIP fabric softener. NEVER hot water — locks oil and carbon-black pigment, yellows polyester.

homeSolutions.agent: AIR-DRY and Inspect Before Any Heat
homeSolutions.instruction: Hang to AIR-DRY. Inspect in daylight. Faint gray shadow = repeat Dawn pass. NEVER hot dryer until cleared — locks residual pigment into polyester. Honest cap: long-wear / waterproof formulas on dyed polyester sometimes leave a faint gray shadow.

steps: 1. Identify standard vs waterproof. Unknown = treat as waterproof.
2. NO hot water. NO hot dryer until cleared.
3. Scrape excess gently with plastic-edge. NEVER rub.
5. Oil-based makeup remover for waterproof — cloth tamp 5 min.
6. Generous Dawn pretreat, cool, 10 min, soft toothbrush.
7. OxiClean cool soak 30 min + cool wash, no softener.
8. AIR-DRY + daylight inspect. No dryer until cleared.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot water or hot dryer was already used (often permanent gray shadow). Stop on waterproof / 24-hour formula on dyed polyester — plateau likely, pro for valuable garments. Stop on specialty / performance polyester (pro). Stop after 3 home cycles without progress.
warnings: NEVER hot water — locks oil and carbon-black pigment
NEVER hot dryer until cleared — yellows polyester
NEVER fabric softener — coats fiber, blocks cleaning
Always scrape gently, NEVER rub — rubbing pushes carbon pigment into fiber
Waterproof mascara needs oil-based remover BEFORE Dawn
Polyester is oleophilic — extended Dawn dwell needed

neverDo: Never rub — pushes carbon-black pigment into polyester surface
Never hot water on mascara + polyester
Never skip the oil-based remover step on waterproof mascara
fiberSensitivities: Polyester is oleophilic — grabs mascara wax + oil tightly
Carbon-black pigment is extremely fine — fiber surface texture grabs it under rubbing
Waterproof mascara: silicone barrier blocks water-based cleaning until removed
Performance / specialty polyester: pro — coatings react unpredictably

materialWarnings: Standard vs waterproof is the fork — different protocol
Scrape gently, never rub — carbon pigment pushes in
No hot water / no hot dryer — polyester locks pigment + yellows
Honest cap: faint gray shadow on waterproof / long-wear formulas

escalation.when: Hot water or hot dryer already used (often permanent gray shadow). Waterproof / 24-hour mascara on dyed polyester (likely plateau). Performance / specialty / dry-clean-only polyester. Rubbing has pushed carbon pigment into fiber surface. 3 home cycles plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Mascara on [white / dyed / athletic / specialty] polyester, formula [standard / waterproof / 24-hour], age [fresh / hours / hot-dried suspected], home-treated with [scrape + cornstarch preload + oil-based remover (waterproof) + Dawn cool + OxiClean cool soak + cool wash + air-dry]. No hot, no rubbing. [Residual gray shadow / hot-dried suspected / waterproof plateau]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with solvent spotting capability for combo / waterproof cosmetic stains

contextQuestions: Fresh, hours old, or hot-dried already?
Has anyone rubbed the stain trying to clean it?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Standard vs waterproof is the entire fork. Waterproof mascara needs the oil-based remover step before Dawn — skipping it means Dawn never reaches the pigment. The carbon-black pigment is the second key issue: it's extremely fine and grabs polyester's surface texture aggressively under rubbing, leaving a permanent gray shadow that can look set when it's just rubbed in. Honest cap on long-wear / waterproof formulas on dyed polyester is faint gray shadow remaining.
